sub_sections.title: Objectives and ambition
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO pursues eight objectives aligned with topic HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02, each defined with measurable and verifiable targets. OBJ-1 (Neuro-symbolic planning engine for autonomous task decomposition): ≥40% improvement in plan success rate over LLM-based baselines (ReAct, Reflexion) on WebArena, SWE-bench, and GAIA for tasks requiring >10 sequential steps (M36). OBJ-2 (Unified adaptive memory architecture): ≥30% improvement in task coherence and factual consistency over context-window-only baselines in sequences exceeding 50 agent steps (M30). OBJ-3 (Decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols): effective coordination of ≥5 heterogeneous agents, ≥25% improvement in joint task completion over independent baselines, and formal proof of protocol convergence (M36). OBJ-8 (External Tool and API Orchestration Layer): typed tool registry supporting ≥30 external tools/APIs, tool invocation success rate ≥95% with verified output validation, mean recovery time from tool failure ≤5s, and provenance traces for 100% of invocations (M36). OBJ-4 (Healthcare demonstrator — clinical decision support): diagnostic reasoning accuracy within 10% of specialist clinician performance on 500 de-identified cases spanning 5 diagnostic domains (M42). OBJ-5 (Manufacturing demonstrator — process optimisation): ≥15% reduction in defect rates AND ≥10% reduction in energy consumption in a pilot factory environment (M42). OBJ-6 (Logistics transfer demonstrator — cross-sector generalisation): ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time AND ≥15% improvement in delivery schedule adherence across 3 supply chain corridors (M42). OBJ-7 (Open framework release and ecosystem contribution): open-source release with ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of release, accepted contributions to the AI-on-demand platform, results validated through ≥2 TEFs, and technology transfer through ≥3 EDIHs (M48). MAESTRO advances beyond LLM-based agent systems (ReAct, AutoGPT-style systems) by co-designing neuro-symbolic planning with formal correctness guarantees, adaptive memory, and formally specified multi-agent coordination into a unified architecture — capabilities currently dispersed across disconnected research communities. Research pillar outputs target TRL 4 by M36; demonstrators (OBJ-4, OBJ-5, OBJ-6) target TRL 5 in operational environments by M42, starting from TRL 2 — appropriate for a RIA advancing foundational agent architectures.

sub_sections.title: Methodology
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO's methodology rests on a co-design principle: all three research pillars and the External Tool and API Orchestration Layer share common data representations, benchmarking protocols, and integration interfaces from project outset. WP2 (Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning Engine, ATU lead) develops the hybrid planning engine through T2-01 (sub-task graph decomposition with formal pre/post-conditions), T2-02 (MCTS and HTN integration with LLM heuristics), and T2-03 (plan monitoring and self-correction), yielding D2-01 (M18) and D2-02 (M36). WP3 (Adaptive Memory Architecture, BIIS lead) delivers the tri-store memory system — working memory, episodic memory, and semantic memory with a learned controller — through T3-01 through T3-03, yielding D3-01 (M12) and D3-02 (M30). WP4 (Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination, CERIA lead) develops formally specified delegation and negotiation protocols with convergence guarantees through T4-01 through T4-03, yielding D4-01 (M18) and D4-02 (M36). WP8 (Integration, Evaluation and Benchmarking, FIIT lead) integrates all pillars and produces the cross-demonstrator benchmark suite (D8-01, M12; D8-02, M42; D8-03, M48). The methodology is genuinely interdisciplinary, combining symbolic AI and formal methods (ATU, CERIA), deep learning and information retrieval (BIIS, BAL), medical informatics (NIHS), industrial engineering and digital twins (ISIA), and AI evaluation methodology (FIIT). MAESTRO builds on and addresses limitations of contemporary agent systems (ReAct, AutoGPT-style frameworks, retrieval-augmented generation) through formal architectural integration rather than incremental extension. This is a technical computer science and engineering research project; social sciences and humanities integration and the gender dimension in R&I content are not applicable to the core system-level agent architecture research. Open science practices include mandatory open-source release of the unified MAESTRO framework (D9-02, M36), open contribution to the AI-on-demand platform, and open publication of results. A Data Management Plan (D1-02) will be delivered by M6 covering FAIR principles for all datasets, including de-identified clinical data (WP5) and logistics operational data (WP7).

claim_statuses.claim_summary: Eight objective IDs with verbatim canonical titles from objectives.json
OBJ-1 target: ≥40% plan success rate over baselines on WebArena/SWE-bench/GAIA (>10 steps, M36)
OBJ-2 target: ≥30% task coherence improvement over context-window-only baselines (50+ steps, M30)
OBJ-3 targets: ≥5 agents coordinated, ≥25% joint task improvement, formal convergence proof (M36)
OBJ-8 targets: ≥30 tools/APIs, ≥95% invocation success, ≤5s recovery, 100% provenance (M36)
OBJ-4 healthcare target: within 10% of specialist on 500 de-identified cases across 5 domains (M42)
OBJ-5 manufacturing targets: ≥15% defect reduction AND ≥10% energy reduction in pilot factory (M42)
OBJ-6 logistics targets: ≥20% disruption recovery AND ≥15% schedule adherence across 3 corridors (M42)
OBJ-7 open framework targets: ≥500 GitHub stars, AI-on-demand, ≥2 TEFs, ≥3 EDIHs (M48)
